.CtxtMenu_MenuClose {  position:absolute;  cursor:pointer;  display:inline-block;  border:2px solid #AAA;  border-radius:18px;  -webkit-border-radius: 18px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 18px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 18px;              /* Konqueror */  font-family: "Courier New", Courier;  font-size:24px;  color:#F0F0F0}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose span {  display:block; background-color:#AAA; border:1.5px solid;  border-radius:18px;  -webkit-border-radius: 18px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 18px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 18px;              /* Konqueror */  line-height:0;  padding:8px 0 6px     /* may need to be browser-specific */}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover {  color:white!important;  border:2px solid #CCC!important}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover span {  background-color:#CCC!important}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover:focus {  outline:none}

.CtxtMenu_Menu {  position:absolute;  background-color:white;  color:black;  width:auto; padding:5px 0px;  border:1px solid #CCCCCC; margin:0; cursor:default;  font: menu; text-align:left; text-indent:0; text-transform:none;  line-height:normal; letter-spacing:normal; word-spacing:normal;  word-wrap:normal; white-space:nowrap; float:none; z-index:201;  border-radius: 5px;                     /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-border-radius: 5px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 5px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 5px;              /* Konqueror */  box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;         /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080; /* Safari 3 & Chrome */  -moz-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;    /* Forefox 3.5 */  -khtml-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;  /* Konqueror */}
.CtxtMenu_MenuItem {  padding: 1px 2em;  background:transparent;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuArrow {  position:absolute; right:.5em; padding-top:.25em; color:#666666;  font-family: null; font-size: .75em}
.CtxtMenu_MenuActive .CtxtMenu_MenuArrow {color:white}
.CtxtMenu_MenuArrow.CtxtMenu_RTL {left:.5em; right:auto}
.CtxtMenu_MenuCheck {  position:absolute; left:.7em;  font-family: null}
.CtxtMenu_MenuCheck.CtxtMenu_RTL { right:.7em; left:auto }
.CtxtMenu_MenuRadioCheck {  position:absolute; left: .7em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuRadioCheck.CtxtMenu_RTL {  right: .7em; left:auto}
.CtxtMenu_MenuInputBox {  padding-left: 1em; right:.5em; color:#666666;  font-family: null;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuInputBox.CtxtMenu_RTL {  left: .1em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuComboBox {  left:.1em; padding-bottom:.5em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuSlider {  left: .1em;}
.CtxtMenu_SliderValue {  position:absolute; right:.1em; padding-top:.25em; color:#333333;  font-size: .75em}
.CtxtMenu_SliderBar {  outline: none; background: #d3d3d3}
.CtxtMenu_MenuLabel {  padding: 1px 2em 3px 1.33em;  font-style:italic}
.CtxtMenu_MenuRule {  border-top: 1px solid #DDDDDD;  margin: 4px 3px;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuDisabled {  color:GrayText}
.CtxtMenu_MenuActive {  background-color: #606872;  color: white;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuDisabled:focus {  background-color: #E8E8E8}
.CtxtMenu_MenuLabel:focus {  background-color: #E8E8E8}
.CtxtMenu_ContextMenu:focus {  outline:none}
.CtxtMenu_ContextMenu .CtxtMenu_MenuItem:focus {  outline:none}
.CtxtMenu_SelectionMenu {  position:relative; float:left;  border-bottom: none; -webkit-box-shadow:none; -webkit-border-radius:0px; }
.CtxtMenu_SelectionItem {  padding-right: 1em;}
.CtxtMenu_Selection {  right: 40%; width:50%; }
.CtxtMenu_SelectionBox {  padding: 0em; max-height:20em; max-width: none;  background-color:#FFFFFF;}
.CtxtMenu_SelectionDivider {  clear: both; border-top: 2px solid #000000;}
.CtxtMenu_Menu .CtxtMenu_MenuClose {  top:-10px; left:-10px}


mjx-container[jax="SVG"] {
  direction: ltr;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] > svg {
  overflow: visible;
  min-height: 1px;
  min-width: 1px;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] > svg a {
  fill: blue;
  stroke: blue;
}

mjx-assistive-mml {
  position: absolute !important;
  top: 0px;
  left: 0px;
  clip: rect(1px, 1px, 1px, 1px);
  padding: 1px 0px 0px 0px !important;
  border: 0px !important;
  display: block !important;
  width: auto !important;
  overflow: hidden !important;
  -webkit-touch-callout: none;
  -webkit-user-select: none;
  -khtml-user-select: none;
  -moz-user-select: none;
  -ms-user-select: none;
  user-select: none;
}

mjx-assistive-mml[display="block"] {
  width: 100% !important;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][display="true"] {
  display: block;
  text-align: center;
  margin: 1em 0;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][display="true"][width="full"] {
  display: flex;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][justify="left"] {
  text-align: left;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][justify="right"] {
  text-align: right;
}

g[data-mml-node="merror"] > g {
  fill: red;
  stroke: red;
}

g[data-mml-node="merror"] > rect[data-background] {
  fill: yellow;
  stroke: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > line[data-line], svg[data-table] > g > line[data-line] {
  stroke-width: 70px;
  fill: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > rect[data-frame], svg[data-table] > g > rect[data-frame] {
  stroke-width: 70px;
  fill: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > .mjx-dashed, svg[data-table] > g > .mjx-dashed {
  stroke-dasharray: 140;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > .mjx-dotted, svg[data-table] > g > .mjx-dotted {
  stroke-linecap: round;
  stroke-dasharray: 0,140;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > g > svg {
  overflow: visible;
}

[jax="SVG"] mjx-tool {
  display: inline-block;
  position: relative;
  width: 0;
  height: 0;
}

[jax="SVG"] mjx-tool > mjx-tip {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
}

mjx-tool > mjx-tip {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: .2em;
  border: 1px solid #888;
  font-size: 70%;
  background-color: #F8F8F8;
  color: black;
  box-shadow: 2px 2px 5px #AAAAAA;
}

g[data-mml-node="maction"][data-toggle] {
  cursor: pointer;
}

mjx-status {
  display: block;
  position: fixed;
  left: 1em;
  bottom: 1em;
  min-width: 25%;
  padding: .2em .4em;
  border: 1px solid #888;
  font-size: 90%;
  background-color: #F8F8F8;
  color: black;
}

foreignObject[data-mjx-xml] {
  font-family: initial;
  line-height: normal;
  overflow: visible;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] path[data-c], mjx-container[jax="SVG"] use[data-c] {
  stroke-width: 3;
}

</style>

<section class="slide split active" style="display: flex; height: 732px; grid-auto-flow: row; gap: 48px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-flow: row;"><div class="col-text"><div class="kick">the report · 15</div><h2>What you get.</h2><div class="body"><div class="compare"><div class="box"><div class="t acc">The field histogram</div>The distribution of local density against the uniform reference — roomy, globally dense, or pocketed — with the global gap and any pockets marked.</div><div class="box"><div class="t acc">The crowding cloud</div>The projection recolored and reshaped by each item’s density (dots → pyramids), drag-to-rotate, with a kNN-edge overlay. Read it from color and shape, not position.</div></div><div class="note"><strong>Honest about its limits.</strong> The field is a k-NN density estimate in cosine units — a <em>rank</em>, not a calibrated density (the cosine→density map is nonlinear in high d), and high-dimensional density estimation suffers distance concentration, hubness, and fixed-k edge bias. Read it as a relative density map and a calibrated pocket detector.</div></div></div><div class="col-fig"><figure class="dg"><svg viewBox="0 0 760 260" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="Crowded points grouped into pockets.   * { box-sizing: border-box; }
  html, body { margin: 0; }
  body {
    background: var(--paper);
    color: var(--ink);
    font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    font-size: 14px;
    line-height: 1.5;
    transition: background .18s, color .18s;
    padding: 0 0 80px;
  }

  header {
    position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10;
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px;
    padding: 14px 24px;
    background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--paper) 88%, transparent);
    backdrop-filter: blur(8px);
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
  }
  header h1 { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; margin: 0; letter-spacing: .02em; }
  header .crumb { font-size: 11px; color: var(--ink-faint); }
  header .crumb a { color: var(--ink-faint); text-decoration: none; }
  header .crumb a:hover { color: var(--accent); }
  header .spacer { flex: 1; }

  main { max-width: 1180px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 24px; }
  section { margin: 34px 0; }
  h2 {
    font-size: 13px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .12em;
    color: var(--ink-faint); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
    padding-bottom: 8px; margin-bottom: 6px;
  }
  .lede { color: var(--ink-soft); font-size: 13px; margin: 4px 0 20px; max-width: 88ch; }
  .lede code { background: var(--panel); padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 12px; }
  .mono { font-family: inherit; }
  .good { color: var(--good); }
  .bad  { color: var(--bad); }
  .faint{ color: var(--ink-faint); }
  .acc  { color: var(--accent); }

  /* sample fact strip */
  .sample {
    border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--panel);
    padding: 16px 20px; margin: 0 0 12px;
    display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px,1fr)); gap: 14px 24px;
  }
  .sample .kv { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; }
  .sample .kv .k { font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: .1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-faint); }
  .sample .kv .v { font-size: 15px; color: var(--ink); }
  .sample .kv .v b { font-weight: 700; }

  /* option card */
  .opt {
    border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--panel);
    padding: 0; margin-bottom: 22px; overflow: hidden;
  }
  .opt > .opt-head {
    display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 12px;
    padding: 14px 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule); background: var(--panel-2);
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .opt-head .num {
    font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .1em; color: var(--paper);
    background: var(--accent); border-radius: 5px; padding: 3px 9px;
  }
  .opt-head .name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--ink); }
  .opt-head .tech { font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--ink-faint); border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 999px; padding: 2px 8px; }
  .opt-head .why { font-size: 12px; color: var(--ink-soft); flex: 1 1 100%; margin-top: 2px; }
  .opt-body { padding: 20px; }
  figure { margin: 0; width: 100%; }
  figure svg { display: block; width: 100%; height: auto; }
  .fig-narrow { max-width: 620px; margin: 0 auto; }
  .fig-mid { max-width: 820px; }

  .reveal {
    margin-top: 14px; font-size: 11.5px; color: var(--ink-soft);
    border: 1px solid var(--rule); padding: 7px 12px;
    background: var(--panel-2); border-radius: 6px;
  }
  .reveal b { color: var(--ink); }

  /* small inline legend under figures */
  .leg { display: flex; gap: 16px; flex-wrap: wrap; font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--ink-faint); margin-top: 12px; }
  .leg span { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }
  .leg i { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 2px; display: inline-block; border: 1px solid transparent; }
  .leg i.g { background: var(--good); } .leg i.r { background: var(--bad); }
  .leg i.a { background: var(--accent); } .leg i.f { background: var(--ink-faint); }
  .leg i.dash { background: transparent; border: 1px dashed var(--ink-faint); }
  .leg i.cache { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--caution); }
  .leg i.c { background: transparent; border: 1px dashed var(--caution); }

  /* ---- isoscore hovercard: a theme-aware, keyboard-reachable explainer popover ---- */
  .hc-fig { position: relative; }
  .hc { position: absolute; z-index: 4; font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
        font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--accent); cursor: help;
        border-bottom: 1px dotted var(--accent); white-space: nowrap; }
  .hc.hc-big { font-size: 19px; font-weight: 700; border-bottom-width: 0; }
  .hc:focus { outline: none; }
  .hc:focus-visible { outline: 1px dashed var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }
  .hc .hc-i { display: inline-block; width: 13px; height: 13px; line-height: 12px; text-align: center;
              border: 1px solid var(--accent); border-radius: 50%; font-size: 9px; font-style: normal;
              font-weight: 700; vertical-align: 2px; margin-left: 5px; }
  .hc-card { position: absolute; top: calc(100% + 8px); right: 0; width: 366px; max-width: 78vw;
             background: var(--panel); color: var(--ink); border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 4px;
             box-shadow: 0 8px 26px rgba(0,0,0,.30); padding: 13px 15px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 400;
             white-space: normal; text-align: left; z-index: 30;
             visibility: hidden; opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-3px); transition: opacity .12s, transform .12s; }
  .hc--up .hc-card { top: auto; bottom: calc(100% + 8px); transform: translateY(3px); }
  .hc:hover .hc-card, .hc:focus-within .hc-card { visibility: visible; opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
  .hc-card .hc-h { color: var(--accent); font-weight: 700; font-size: 13px; }
  .hc-card .hc-sub { color: var(--ink-faint); font-size: 9.5px; margin: 2px 0 9px; }
  .hc-card p { margin: 0 0 9px; line-height: 1.5; color: var(--ink-soft); }
  .hc-card .hc-math { border-top: 1px solid var(--rule-soft); padding-top: 9px; font-size: 10.5px; line-height: 1.85; }
  .hc-card .hc-intro { color: var(--ink-faint); font-size: 9.5px; margin-bottom: 5px; }
  .hc-card .hc-eq { color: var(--ink); }
  .hc-card .hc-eq .hc-note { color: var(--ink-faint); }
  .hc-card .hc-foot { margin-top: 10px; color: var(--accent); font-size: 10px;
                      border-top: 1px solid var(--rule-soft); padding-top: 8px; }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hc-card { transition: none; } }

  /* ---- "how to read" hovercard: the same explainer popover, anchored in a card header ---- */
  .opt-head .hc-head { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;
    font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600;
    color: var(--ink-faint); border-bottom: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: help; }
  .opt-head .hc-head:hover, .opt-head .hc-head:focus-within { color: var(--accent); }
  .opt-head .hc-head .hc-i { margin-left: 0; width: 14px; height: 14px; line-height: 13px; cursor: help; }
  .opt-head .hc-head:hover .hc-i, .opt-head .hc-head:focus-within .hc-i {
    background: var(--accent); color: var(--paper); }
  .opt-head .hc-head .hc-card { top: calc(100% + 8px); left: 0; right: auto;
    text-transform: none; letter-spacing: normal; font-weight: 400; }
  .hc-card .hc-look { color: var(--ink); }
  .hc-card .hc-bad { color: var(--bad); } .hc-card .hc-good { color: var(--good); }

  svg text { font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; }

  footer { max-width: 1180px; margin: 40px auto 0; padding: 18px 24px; color: var(--ink-faint); font-size: 11px; border-top: 1px solid var(--rule); }


  /* ---- the COLOUR SWATCH DROPDOWN (16 Console themes) — mirrors .dt-cd ---- */
  .theme-pick-label { font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: .12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-faint); }
  .dt-cd { position: relative; display: inline-flex; }
  .dt-cd-trigger { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
    border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 5px; padding: 5px 9px;
    background: var(--panel); color: var(--ink-soft); cursor: pointer;
    font: inherit; font-size: 11px; transition: border-color .15s, color .15s; }
  .dt-cd-trigger:hover { color: var(--ink); border-color: var(--accent); }
  .dt-cd-trigger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }
  .dt-cd-name { color: var(--ink); white-space: nowrap; }
  .dt-cd-caret { color: var(--ink-faint); font-size: 8px; transition: transform .15s; }
  .dt-cd.dt-cd-open .dt-cd-caret { transform: rotate(180deg); }
  .dt-swatch-strip { display: inline-flex; border-radius: 3px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--rule); }
  .dt-swatch { display: inline-block; width: 11px; height: 13px; }
  .dt-swatch-strip-sm .dt-swatch { width: 9px; height: 11px; }
  .dt-cd-list { display: none; position: absolute; top: calc(100% + 5px); right: 0; z-index: 60;
    min-width: 200px; max-height: 70vh; overflow-y: auto;
    background: var(--panel); border: 1px solid var(--rule); border-radius: 6px;
    box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.28); padding: 4px; }
  .dt-cd.dt-cd-open .dt-cd-list { display: block; }
  .dt-cd-option { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 5px 7px; border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer; color: var(--ink-soft); transition: background .12s, color .12s; }
  .dt-cd-option .dt-cd-name { font-size: 10.5px; }
  .dt-cd-option:hover, .dt-cd-option.dt-cd-active { background: var(--rule-soft); color: var(--ink); }
  .dt-cd-option[aria-selected="true"] .dt-cd-name { color: var(--accent); }

*{box-sizing:border-box}
html,body{height:100%;margin:0}
body{background:var(--paper);color:var(--ink);font-family:ui-monospace,"SF Mono",Menlo,Consolas,monospace;overflow:hidden;transition:background .18s,color .18s;}
.topbar{position:fixed;top:0;left:0;right:0;height:44px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:13px;padding:0 24px;z-index:60;}
.topbar .wm{font-weight:700;letter-spacing:.04em;color:var(--ink);font-size:15px;}
.topbar .crumb{font-size:11px;color:var(--ink-faint);letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;}
.topbar .spacer{flex:1;}
.topbar .count{font-size:12px;color:var(--ink-faint);font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;}
.theme-pick-label{font-size:11px;color:var(--ink-faint);letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase;}
.progress{position:fixed;bottom:0;left:0;height:3px;background:var(--accent);width:0;z-index:60;transition:width .25s;}
.hint{position:fixed;bottom:11px;right:22px;font-size:10.5px;color:var(--ink-faint);letter-spacing:.06em;z-index:55;}
.stage{position:fixed;transform-origin:0 0;width:1280px;height:800px;background:var(--paper);}
.slide{position:absolute;inset:0;padding:42px 92px 48px;display:none;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;overflow:hidden;}
.slide.active{display:flex;animation:fin .28s ease both;}
@keyframes fin{from{opacity:0;transform:translateY(8px)}to{opacity:1;transform:none}}
.slide .kick{font-size:13px;letter-spacing:.2em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--ink-faint);margin-bottom:12px;}
.slide h2{font-size:42px;font-weight:700;line-height:1.12;margin:0 0 .5em;color:var(--ink);max-width:1090px;}
.slide .accent{color:var(--accent);}
.slide .body{font-size:19px;color:var(--ink-soft);line-height:1.6;max-width:1050px;}
.slide .body p{margin:0 0 .7em;} .slide .body b,.slide .body strong{color:var(--ink);}
.slide ul{margin:.3em 0;padding-left:1.1em;} .slide li{margin:.34em 0;}
.slide .lead{font-size:21px;}
figure.dg{margin:8px 0 0;text-align:center;}
figure.dg svg{display:block;max-height:404px;width:auto;max-width:1010px;margin:0 auto;}
figure.dg figcaption{font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-faint);margin-top:9px;max-width:990px;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;line-height:1.5;}
.note{border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--panel-2);border-radius:8px;padding:11px 15px;margin-top:14px;font-size:15px;color:var(--ink-soft);line-height:1.55;max-width:1050px;}
.note strong{color:var(--ink);}
.compare{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:16px;margin:10px 0;max-width:1010px;}
.compare .box{border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--panel);border-radius:8px;padding:14px 16px;font-size:16px;color:var(--ink-soft);line-height:1.5;}
.compare .box .t{font-weight:700;margin-bottom:6px;} .compare .box .t.good{color:var(--good);} .compare .box .t.bad{color:var(--bad);} .compare .box .t.acc{color:var(--accent);}
.eq{font-size:22px;color:var(--ink);margin:.35em 0;}
.num-list{list-style:none;padding:0;counter-reset:n;max-width:1080px;}
.num-list li{counter-increment:n;position:relative;padding-left:2.5em;margin:.5em 0;font-size:17px;color:var(--ink-soft);line-height:1.5;}
.num-list li::before{content:counter(n);position:absolute;left:0;top:.05em;font-weight:700;color:var(--paper);background:var(--accent);border-radius:5px;padding:1px 9px;font-size:.82em;}
.num-list li b{color:var(--ink);}
.slide.title{justify-content:center;align-items:flex-start;}
.slide .wm{font-size:148px;font-weight:700;line-height:.86;color:var(--ink);letter-spacing:-.02em;}
.slide .tagline{font-size:32px;color:var(--ink-soft);margin-top:.32em;max-width:1010px;line-height:1.2;}
.slide .title-sub{font-size:16px;color:var(--ink-faint);margin-top:1.25em;max-width:800px;line-height:1.6;}

  /* ---- split layout: text beside a diagram (kills the stacked top-clip) ---- */
  .slide.split{flex-direction:row;align-items:center;gap:48px;padding:38px 74px;}
  .slide.split .col-text{flex:1 1 0;min-width:0;max-width:560px;}
  .slide.split .col-fig{flex:1 1 0;min-width:0;display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;justify-content:center;}
  .slide.split h2{font-size:30px;line-height:1.15;margin:0 0 .5em;}
  .slide.split .body{font-size:16px;line-height:1.55;max-width:none;}
  .slide.split .body p{margin:0 0 .62em;}
  .slide.split .lead{font-size:17px;}
  .slide.split .compare{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:10px;max-width:none;margin:8px 0;}
  .slide.split .compare .box{font-size:13.5px;padding:11px 14px;}
  .slide.split .note{font-size:13px;padding:9px 13px;margin-top:11px;max-width:none;}
  .slide.split .num-list{max-width:none;}
  .slide.split .num-list li{font-size:13.5px;margin:.4em 0;padding-left:2.3em;line-height:1.45;}
  .slide.split .eq{font-size:19px;margin:.2em 0 .55em;}
  .col-fig figure.dg{margin:0;width:100%;}
  .col-fig figure.dg svg{max-height:444px;max-width:100%;margin:0 auto;}
  .col-fig figcaption{font-size:12px;color:var(--ink-faint);margin-top:11px;line-height:1.5;max-width:560px;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;text-align:left;}
  /* ---- cv3d vanilla-canvas 3D component (auto-spin, drag-rotate) ---- */
  .cv3d{position:relative;width:100%;max-width:560px;cursor:grab;touch-action:none;margin:0 auto;}
  .cv3d:active{cursor:grabbing;}
  .cv3d-canvas{display:block;width:100%;border:1px solid var(--rule-soft);border-radius:8px;background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--panel) 55%, transparent);}
  .cv3d-tabs{display:flex;gap:6px;margin-bottom:8px;justify-content:center;}
  .cv3d-tab{font:inherit;font-size:11px;padding:3px 12px;border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:999px;background:var(--panel);color:var(--ink-soft);cursor:pointer;}
  .cv3d-tab.on{background:var(--accent);color:var(--paper);border-color:var(--accent);font-weight:700;}
  .cv3d-tab:hover{color:var(--ink);}
  /* ---- duo layout: a 2D schematic beside its 3D view (same concept, both modalities) ---- */
  .slide.duo{flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;align-items:center;padding:34px 74px;}
  .slide.duo .duo-head{width:100%;max-width:1100px;}
  .slide.duo h2{font-size:29px;line-height:1.14;margin:0 0 .35em;}
  .slide.duo .body{font-size:15px;line-height:1.5;max-width:1100px;}
  .slide.duo .body p{margin:0;}
  .slide.duo .duo-figs{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:36px;width:100%;max-width:1100px;align-items:center;margin-top:14px;}
  .slide.duo .duo-figs figure.dg{margin:0;width:100%;}
  .slide.duo .duo-figs svg{width:100%;height:auto;max-height:344px;}
  .slide.duo .duo-figs .cv3d{max-width:100%;}
  .slide.duo .duo-figs figcaption{font-size:11.5px;line-height:1.45;text-align:center;margin:9px auto 0;max-width:none;}
  /* ---- method pipeline: one diagram per algorithm step, left to right ---- */
  .slide.method{flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;align-items:center;padding:38px 48px;}
  .slide.method h2{font-size:34px;line-height:1.12;margin:0 0 .15em;}
  .slide.method .pipeline{display:flex;align-items:stretch;justify-content:center;gap:4px;width:100%;max-width:1190px;margin-top:18px;}
  .slide.method .step{flex:1 1 0;min-width:0;display:flex;flex-direction:column;border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;background:var(--panel);padding:9px 10px 11px;}
  .slide.method .step figure.dg{margin:0;width:100%;}
  .slide.method .step figure.dg svg{display:block;width:100%;height:auto;max-height:none;}
  .slide.method .step .cap{margin-top:7px;}
  .slide.method .step .cap .sb{display:inline-block;font-weight:700;color:var(--paper);background:var(--accent);border-radius:5px;padding:0 7px;font-size:11px;margin-right:6px;}
  .slide.method .step .cap b{font-size:13px;color:var(--ink);}
  .slide.method .step .cap p{font-size:11px;color:var(--ink-soft);margin:5px 0 0;line-height:1.42;}
  .slide.method .parrow{display:flex;align-items:center;color:var(--ink-faint);font-size:17px;flex:0 0 auto;}
  /* ---- title slide as split ---- */
  .slide.title.split{align-items:center;justify-content:center;}
  .slide.title.split .col-text{max-width:600px;}
  .slide.split .wm{font-size:120px;}
  .slide.split .tagline{font-size:27px;margin-top:.3em;}
  .slide.split .title-sub{font-size:14.5px;margin-top:1.05em;max-width:560px;}
  /* research-preview status tag in the topbar — a quiet two-line mark by the
     wordmark; faint, monospace, a hairline divider, no fill/glow/animation. */
  .topbar .respreview{display:inline-flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;align-self:center;pointer-events:none;user-select:none;font-size:8.5px;line-height:1.2;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.1em;color:var(--ink-faint);border-left:1px solid var(--rule);padding-left:9px;}
  .topbar .respreview span{display:block;white-space:nowrap;}

.CtxtMenu_InfoClose {  top:.2em; right:.2em;}
.CtxtMenu_InfoContent {  overflow:auto; text-align:left; font-size:80%;  padding:.4em .6em; border:1px inset; margin:1em 0px;  max-height:20em; max-width:30em; background-color:#EEEEEE;  white-space:normal;}
.CtxtMenu_Info.CtxtMenu_MousePost {outline:none;}
.CtxtMenu_Info {  position:fixed; left:50%; width:auto; text-align:center;  border:3px outset; padding:1em 2em; background-color:#DDDDDD;  color:black;  cursor:default; font-family:message-box; font-size:120%;  font-style:normal; text-indent:0; text-transform:none;  line-height:normal; letter-spacing:normal; word-spacing:normal;  word-wrap:normal; white-space:nowrap; float:none; z-index:201;  border-radius: 15px;                     /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-border-radius:15px;               /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius:15px;                  /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius:15px;                /* Konqueror */  box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;         /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080; /* Safari 3 & Chrome */  -moz-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;    /* Forefox 3.5 */  -khtml-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;  /* Konqueror */  filter:progid:DXImageTransform.Microsoft.dropshadow(OffX=2, OffY=2, Color="gray", Positive="true"); /* IE */}

.CtxtMenu_MenuClose {  position:absolute;  cursor:pointer;  display:inline-block;  border:2px solid #AAA;  border-radius:18px;  -webkit-border-radius: 18px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 18px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 18px;              /* Konqueror */  font-family: "Courier New", Courier;  font-size:24px;  color:#F0F0F0}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose span {  display:block; background-color:#AAA; border:1.5px solid;  border-radius:18px;  -webkit-border-radius: 18px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 18px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 18px;              /* Konqueror */  line-height:0;  padding:8px 0 6px     /* may need to be browser-specific */}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover {  color:white!important;  border:2px solid #CCC!important}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover span {  background-color:#CCC!important}
.CtxtMenu_MenuClose:hover:focus {  outline:none}

.CtxtMenu_Menu {  position:absolute;  background-color:white;  color:black;  width:auto; padding:5px 0px;  border:1px solid #CCCCCC; margin:0; cursor:default;  font: menu; text-align:left; text-indent:0; text-transform:none;  line-height:normal; letter-spacing:normal; word-spacing:normal;  word-wrap:normal; white-space:nowrap; float:none; z-index:201;  border-radius: 5px;                     /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-border-radius: 5px;             /* Safari and Chrome */  -moz-border-radius: 5px;                /* Firefox */  -khtml-border-radius: 5px;              /* Konqueror */  box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;         /* Opera 10.5 and IE9 */  -webkit-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080; /* Safari 3 & Chrome */  -moz-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;    /* Forefox 3.5 */  -khtml-box-shadow:0px 10px 20px #808080;  /* Konqueror */}
.CtxtMenu_MenuItem {  padding: 1px 2em;  background:transparent;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuArrow {  position:absolute; right:.5em; padding-top:.25em; color:#666666;  font-family: null; font-size: .75em}
.CtxtMenu_MenuActive .CtxtMenu_MenuArrow {color:white}
.CtxtMenu_MenuArrow.CtxtMenu_RTL {left:.5em; right:auto}
.CtxtMenu_MenuCheck {  position:absolute; left:.7em;  font-family: null}
.CtxtMenu_MenuCheck.CtxtMenu_RTL { right:.7em; left:auto }
.CtxtMenu_MenuRadioCheck {  position:absolute; left: .7em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuRadioCheck.CtxtMenu_RTL {  right: .7em; left:auto}
.CtxtMenu_MenuInputBox {  padding-left: 1em; right:.5em; color:#666666;  font-family: null;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuInputBox.CtxtMenu_RTL {  left: .1em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuComboBox {  left:.1em; padding-bottom:.5em;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuSlider {  left: .1em;}
.CtxtMenu_SliderValue {  position:absolute; right:.1em; padding-top:.25em; color:#333333;  font-size: .75em}
.CtxtMenu_SliderBar {  outline: none; background: #d3d3d3}
.CtxtMenu_MenuLabel {  padding: 1px 2em 3px 1.33em;  font-style:italic}
.CtxtMenu_MenuRule {  border-top: 1px solid #DDDDDD;  margin: 4px 3px;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuDisabled {  color:GrayText}
.CtxtMenu_MenuActive {  background-color: #606872;  color: white;}
.CtxtMenu_MenuDisabled:focus {  background-color: #E8E8E8}
.CtxtMenu_MenuLabel:focus {  background-color: #E8E8E8}
.CtxtMenu_ContextMenu:focus {  outline:none}
.CtxtMenu_ContextMenu .CtxtMenu_MenuItem:focus {  outline:none}
.CtxtMenu_SelectionMenu {  position:relative; float:left;  border-bottom: none; -webkit-box-shadow:none; -webkit-border-radius:0px; }
.CtxtMenu_SelectionItem {  padding-right: 1em;}
.CtxtMenu_Selection {  right: 40%; width:50%; }
.CtxtMenu_SelectionBox {  padding: 0em; max-height:20em; max-width: none;  background-color:#FFFFFF;}
.CtxtMenu_SelectionDivider {  clear: both; border-top: 2px solid #000000;}
.CtxtMenu_Menu .CtxtMenu_MenuClose {  top:-10px; left:-10px}


mjx-container[jax="SVG"] {
  direction: ltr;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] > svg {
  overflow: visible;
  min-height: 1px;
  min-width: 1px;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] > svg a {
  fill: blue;
  stroke: blue;
}

mjx-assistive-mml {
  position: absolute !important;
  top: 0px;
  left: 0px;
  clip: rect(1px, 1px, 1px, 1px);
  padding: 1px 0px 0px 0px !important;
  border: 0px !important;
  display: block !important;
  width: auto !important;
  overflow: hidden !important;
  -webkit-touch-callout: none;
  -webkit-user-select: none;
  -khtml-user-select: none;
  -moz-user-select: none;
  -ms-user-select: none;
  user-select: none;
}

mjx-assistive-mml[display="block"] {
  width: 100% !important;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][display="true"] {
  display: block;
  text-align: center;
  margin: 1em 0;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][display="true"][width="full"] {
  display: flex;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][justify="left"] {
  text-align: left;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"][justify="right"] {
  text-align: right;
}

g[data-mml-node="merror"] > g {
  fill: red;
  stroke: red;
}

g[data-mml-node="merror"] > rect[data-background] {
  fill: yellow;
  stroke: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > line[data-line], svg[data-table] > g > line[data-line] {
  stroke-width: 70px;
  fill: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > rect[data-frame], svg[data-table] > g > rect[data-frame] {
  stroke-width: 70px;
  fill: none;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > .mjx-dashed, svg[data-table] > g > .mjx-dashed {
  stroke-dasharray: 140;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > .mjx-dotted, svg[data-table] > g > .mjx-dotted {
  stroke-linecap: round;
  stroke-dasharray: 0,140;
}

g[data-mml-node="mtable"] > g > svg {
  overflow: visible;
}

[jax="SVG"] mjx-tool {
  display: inline-block;
  position: relative;
  width: 0;
  height: 0;
}

[jax="SVG"] mjx-tool > mjx-tip {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
}

mjx-tool > mjx-tip {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: .2em;
  border: 1px solid #888;
  font-size: 70%;
  background-color: #F8F8F8;
  color: black;
  box-shadow: 2px 2px 5px #AAAAAA;
}

g[data-mml-node="maction"][data-toggle] {
  cursor: pointer;
}

mjx-status {
  display: block;
  position: fixed;
  left: 1em;
  bottom: 1em;
  min-width: 25%;
  padding: .2em .4em;
  border: 1px solid #888;
  font-size: 90%;
  background-color: #F8F8F8;
  color: black;
}

foreignObject[data-mjx-xml] {
  font-family: initial;
  line-height: normal;
  overflow: visible;
}

mjx-container[jax="SVG"] path[data-c], mjx-container[jax="SVG"] use[data-c] {
  stroke-width: 3;
}

<style id="dia-theme">
:root {
  --dia-paper: rgb(30, 31, 28);
  --dia-ink: rgb(201, 202, 191);
  --dia-ink-soft: rgb(150, 151, 142);
  --dia-accent: rgb(249, 38, 114);
  --dia-rule: rgb(61, 62, 57);
  --dia-face-display: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  --dia-face-body: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  --dia-face-label: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  --dia-scale-1: 1cqw;
  --dia-scale-2: 1.17cqw;
  --dia-scale-3: 1.4cqw;
  --dia-scale-4: 3.71cqw;
  --dia-scale-5: 10.6cqw;
  --dia-scale-6: 13.25cqw;
  --dia-scale-7: 16.56cqw;
  --dia-gap: 4.24cqw;
  --dia-pad: 5.78%;
}
section.dia-slide {
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
  /* the padding lives INSIDE the ratio — content-box sizing would add it
   * on top and make every padded slide taller than 16:9 */
  box-sizing: border-box;
  /* a slide is one screen: content that runs long clips, exactly as the
   * imported originals clipped it, instead of spilling over the next slide */
  overflow: hidden;
  container-type: inline-size; /* cqw-sized type scales with the slide */
  position: relative; /* slides anchor absolutely-positioned decorations */
  background: var(--dia-paper);
  color: var(--dia-ink);
  padding: var(--dia-pad);
  font-family: var(--dia-face-body);
}
.dia-kicker { font-family: var(--dia-face-label); font-size: var(--dia-scale-1);
  letter-spacing: .14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--dia-accent); }
.dia-title { font-family: var(--dia-face-display); font-size: var(--dia-scale-5);
  line-height: 1.14; font-weight: 700; margin: 0; }
.dia-body { font-size: var(--dia-scale-2); line-height: 1.55; color: var(--dia-ink-soft); }
.dia-caption { font-family: var(--dia-face-label); font-size: var(--dia-scale-1);
  color: var(--dia-ink-soft); }
.dia-columns { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: var(--dia-gap); }
.dia-stack { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: calc(var(--dia-gap) / 2); }
.dia-figure { align-self: center; }
.dia-scene { width: 100%; }
.dia-scene .dia-node-shape { fill: var(--dia-node-fill, var(--dia-paper)); stroke: var(--dia-node-stroke, var(--dia-ink)); stroke-width: var(--dia-node-stroke-w, 1.3); }
.dia-scene .dia-node-label { font: 12px var(--dia-face-body); fill: var(--dia-node-ink, var(--dia-ink)); }
.dia-scene .dia-edge-path { stroke: var(--dia-edge-stroke, var(--dia-ink)); stroke-width: var(--dia-edge-w, 1.2); fill: none; color: var(--dia-edge-stroke, var(--dia-ink)); }
.dia-scene .dia-edge-label { font: 10px var(--dia-face-label); fill: var(--dia-edge-ink, var(--dia-ink-soft)); }
.dia-scene [data-dia-emphasis] .dia-node-shape { stroke: var(--dia-accent); stroke-width: 2; }
.dia-draw { fill: none; stroke: var(--dia-ink); stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; }
</style>
</head>

letter-spacing: 0.2em;">a field guide · density, crowding, and the pockets a global number can’t see</div><h1 class="dia-title" style="line-height: 0.86; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">ambit</h1><div class="dia-body" style="font-size: 2.39cqw; <section class="dia-slide"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the problem · 01</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">An embedding earns its keep only if <span class="accent">things you can tell apart stay apart</span>, and <span class="accent">things that belong together stay close</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">You embed a dataset; one question decides whether retrieval works: <b>can the space keep its items apart — and where it can’t, which items?</b></p><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">The off-the-shelf answers are single global numbers — mean cosine, IsoScore, effective rank — taken over the whole cloud. But a dataset is rarely uniform. One average can call a space healthy while a sub-population has crammed into a crowded cone, or call it broken when only a couple of cluster directions are stretched. We want a measure that is <b>local</b>, and honest about where geometry stops and the task takes over.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><svg viewBox="0 0 620 300" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="A dataset that is mostly diffuse with one small dense pocket; <section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the tension · 02</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Two jobs that pull in <span class="accent">opposite directions</span>.</h2><div class="dia-body" style="color: rgb(166, 226, 46); font-weight: 700;">recall</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">Related items must sit at <em>higher</em> cosine than unrelated ones, so nearest-neighbor search actually <b style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">finds</b> them.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-accent); font-weight: 700;">resolution / precision</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">Unrelated items must sit at <em>low</em> cosine, so you can <b style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">rank</b> them and impostors don’t crowd the top.</div><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">Push related items together for recall and you compress the very angular room you need to rank them apart. The whole game is grouping at the <b>right granularity</b> — tight enough to retrieve a concept together, loose enough that its members stay distinguishable.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><svg viewBox="0 0 620 300" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="Two opposing forces. <span style="color:var(--bad)">resolution</span> needs them spaced enough to rank. Spend all the room on one and you starve the other.</div></div></div></section>

opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 8.5px;">●</tspan> related (should be retrieved)   <tspan fill="var(--ink-faint)" style="fill: rgb(143, 144, 138); opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 8.5px;">●</tspan> unrelated   <tspan fill="var(--caution)" style="fill: rgb(230, 219, 116); opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 8.5px;">◌</tspan> top-k boundary</text></svg><div class="dia-body" style="margin-top: auto;">Over-grouped: relatives and impostors both jam the query → precision dies. Balanced: relatives inside the top-k boundary, impostors outside. Under-grouped: relatives scatter past it → recall dies.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);"><strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Isotropy</strong> is how much angular room is in play; <strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">clustering</strong> spends it to buy recall; <strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">resolution</strong> is the room left to keep items apart (precision). “More isotropic” is not the goal — a perfectly uniform space has no grouping at all. Good geometry is <em>structured</em>: clusters spread over the sphere that stay internally roomy.</div></section>

text-anchor: middle;">cosine similarity</text></svg><div class="dia-body" style="margin-top: auto;">Not all-small cosines (kills recall) nor all-large (kills resolution) — a <em>gap</em>: the related-pair distribution high, the unrelated-pair distribution low and well resolved, with daylight between. <b>Crowding is when the gap closes.</b></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the stage · 05</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Normalize, and an item is a <span class="accent">direction</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">L2-normalize and an embedding is a point on the unit sphere; cosine similarity is the cosine of the angle between two directions. Length is gone — only orientation remains.</p><div class="dia-body" style="color: rgb(166, 226, 46); font-weight: 700;">isotropic</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">Directions spread over the whole sphere. Two random items are almost perpendicular (cos ≈ 0). Lots of room; high resolution.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-accent); font-weight: 700;">anisotropic — the “cone”</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">Directions pile into a narrow patch. Even unrelated items share a high cosine. No room; low resolution.</div></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-columns" style="grid-template-columns: 72fr 44fr;"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-island" data-dia-island=""><button type="button" class="cv3d-tab on">isotropic</button></div></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-island" data-dia-island=""><button type="button" class="cv3d-tab">cone</button></div></div></div><div class="dia-figure"><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="figure (canvas snapshot)" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; width: 44.79cqw;"></div><div class="dia-body">Switch the tabs. <b>Isotropic</b>: directions cover the sphere, two random items are near-perpendicular (cos ≈ 0) — lots of room. <b>Cone</b>: directions pile into one patch, even unrelated items share a high cosine — no room. Drag to rotate.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the stage · 06</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">In 768 dimensions, <span class="accent">“random” means “orthogonal.”</span></h2><div class="dia-columns" style="grid-template-columns: 206fr 67fr;"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-body"><svg var(--dia-ink);">Fix one item. Almost all of a high-dimensional sphere’s area lies in a thin band <b>90° away</b> from it, so a random direction is almost surely near-orthogonal (cos ≈ 0). The random-pair cosine collapses to a needle-thin spike at 0 — <em>concentration of measure</em>.</p><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">Three consequences: <b>“similar” is a tiny window</b> (cos = 0.3 is already 8σ out); <b>a real cluster must be very tight to exist at all</b>; and <b>the average is owned by the diffuse background</b> — the seed of every global-number failure.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><svg viewBox="0 0 560 360" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="A sphere cross-section with a fixed point at the north pole; Few directions land near its pole; nearly all sit at ≈ 90°.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">what breaks · 07</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">The pathology you’ve read about: the <span class="accent">cone effect</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">Representation degeneration (Gao 2019; Ethayarajh 2019): during training the whole cloud drifts toward one cone, so cosine becomes nearly useless — everything is similar to everything.</p><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">That is the standard story, and the standard fixes (mean-centering, all-but-the-top, whitening) target it. But it pictures the dataset as a <b>single uniform cloud</b>. Real datasets are not.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="figure (canvas snapshot)" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; width: 44.79cqw;"></div><div class="dia-body">Representation degeneration: the whole cloud drifts into a single cone, so every pair shares a high cosine and ranking collapses. The standard fixes target this picture — but it assumes one uniform cloud.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">what breaks · 08</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Crowding is almost always <span class="accent">local</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">A real dataset is a mix of roomy and crowded regions. Because almost every pair is a near-orthogonal background pair, a mean over pairs — or a covariance summed over points — is <b>dominated by the diffuse majority</b>. A small, intensely crowded pocket barely moves it.</p><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">So one global number can call the space healthy while a sub-population is unusable — and it cannot tell you <b>where</b>. Our instruments are global; the problem is local.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="figure (canvas snapshot)" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; width: 44.79cqw;"></div><div class="dia-body">A real space: a roomy background with a couple of dense pockets buried in it. A mean over pairs, or a covariance summed over points, is dominated by the diffuse majority — the pockets barely register, and never their location.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the trap · 09</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.56cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Why a global number lies — the <span class="accent">dandelion</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">Picture a <b>dandelion</b>: dozens of thin cones radiating from the center. Each cone is internally crowded — its members nearly collinear, unusable — yet the cones point <em>everywhere</em>. Average over all pairs and the within-cone crowding cancels against the between-cone spread: the cloud as a whole looks <b>isotropic</b>, the global number reports “healthy,” and every cone is broken where the scalar cannot see.</p><div class="dia-columns" style="grid-template-columns: 392fr 507fr;"><div class="dia-stack"><svg viewBox="0 0 340 230" width="360" role="img" aria-label="A dandelion of thin cones radiating from a center with a dashed circular envelope." style="opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; max-width: 100%; height: auto; width: 34.64cqw;" class="dia-scene">

Drag to rotate: the cones fill every direction while each stays a tight sliver. Locally crowded, globally round.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the trap · 10</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">A Simpson’s paradox of <span class="accent">isotropy</span>.</h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">Isotropy is a <b>non-collapsible</b> functional of the covariance: the global score is <em>not</em> any weighted average of the per-cluster scores, and it can flip in either direction. “Is this space anisotropic?” has no answer until you say <em>at what stratification</em>. The lesson is Simpson’s: never trust a marginal; condition on the grouping first.</p><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);"><strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">One honest caveat.</strong> The dandelion is globally isotropic only when the cones are numerous (more than the dimension). With <em>few</em> clusters, </div><div class="dia-body"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="7.509ex" height="1.902ex" role="img" focusable="false" viewBox="0 -683 3318.8 840.8" opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; font-family: ui-monospace, "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 9px; text-anchor: middle;">whole mode shifted up</text></svg><div class="dia-body" style="margin-top: auto;">One mode at the floor → roomy. The whole mode shifted up vs the uniform reference → globally dense. A separated high mode across a valley → a crowded pocket; several bumps → several pockets. A single average can’t tell these apart; the distribution can.</div></section>

Consolas, monospace; font-size: 9.5px;">roomy</text></svg><div class="dia-body" style="margin-top: auto; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; max-width: 56.77cqw;">The data field against a density-matched uniform reference. The cutoff is the reference’s own upper tail; a separated pocket is flagged when it passes that tail — the threshold comes from the reference, not from the data’s own bulk.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);"><strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Why this is sound.</strong> The cutoff is keyed to <em>denser than uniform</em>, so a small but cleanly separated pocket is flagged however short its bump — and a uniformly dense space yields none, its tail simply matching the reference’s. The threshold recalibrates itself at each scale.</div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">the report · 15</div><h2 class="dia-title" style="font-size: 2.65cqw; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">What you get.</h2><div class="dia-body" style="color: rgb(102, 217, 239); font-weight: 700;">The field histogram</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">The distribution of local density against the uniform reference — roomy, globally dense, or pocketed — with the global gap and any pockets marked.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: rgb(102, 217, 239); font-weight: 700;">The crowding cloud</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);">The projection recolored and reshaped by each item’s density (dots → pyramids), drag-to-rotate, with a kNN-edge overlay. Read it from color and shape, not position.</div><div class="dia-body" style="color: var(--dia-ink);"><strong style="color: rgb(248, 248, 242);">Honest about its limits.</strong> The field is a k-NN density estimate in cosine units — a <em>rank</em>, not a calibrated density (the cosine→density map is nonlinear in high d), and high-dimensional density estimation suffers distance concentration, hubness, and fixed-k edge bias. Read it as a relative density map and a calibrated pocket detector.</div></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><svg viewBox="0 0 760 260" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="Crowded points grouped into pockets. Read crowding from color and shape, not screen position.</div></div></div></section>

<section class="dia-slide" style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;"><div class="dia-columns"><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-kicker" style="color: rgb(143, 144, 138);">in one breath · 16</div><h2 class="dia-body"><span class="accent">ambit.</span></h2><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">Embeddings are directions on a sphere; crowding is a region where items get squeezed together; it is almost always <em>local</em>.</p><p class="dia-body" style="font-size: var(--dia-scale-3); color: var(--dia-ink);">So measure crowding as the <b>local density</b> of each item’s neighborhood, calibrate it against a uniform reference, and report the <b>distribution</b> of that field: its modes, the mass in them, and <b>where</b> they are. That describes the anisotropy in a way that a single number cannot.</p></div><div class="dia-stack"><div class="dia-figure"><svg viewBox="0 0 486 448" width="100%" height="auto" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" role="img" aria-label="A dandelion drawn in line art: a seed head of fine radiating spokes, each tipped with a pappus tuft, inside a faint round envelope; a few seeds drift off on the wind in the accent color, the items that have lost their room." style="opacity: 1; fill-opacity: 1; stroke-opacity: 1; max-width: 100%; height: auto; width: 35.95cqw;"><circle cx="204.0" cy="190.0" r="156.0" fill="none" stroke="var(--rule)" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 5" opacity="0.55" style="fill: none;
